title: autobench.sandbox.stderr v1
description: Per-case sandbox stderr excerpt emitted whenever a non-OK error-class verdict (CE/RE/TLE/MLE) fires. Carries the first 200 chars of the sandboxed process's stderr so a downstream consumer can correlate verdicts with the actual error message without parsing case_result.generated_code by hand. Source: /autobench. See bead nervous-bus-bns.

iteration.description: RSI iteration number (0 when not running in an RSI loop)

verdict.description: Error-class verdict that triggered this emission. OK and WA never emit on this channel.

stderr_excerpt.description: First 200 characters of the sandboxed process's stderr

exit_code.description: Process exit code if known; null when unavailable (e.g. generation-side errors)

language.description: Programming language for this case (python, cpp, go, glsl, ...)
